weather.tip: Mostly clear right now with only a 31% rain chance left in the day, so the evening commute should stay dry, but it dips to 20°C by early morning — a light jacket helps if you're out late.

around.headline: Family of four found dead in suspected murder-suicide in Kottigepalya
around.summary: Police say a man killed three family members before dying by suicide at their Kottigepalya home; the case is under investigation. If you or someone you know is in crisis, the Kiran mental health helpline (1800-599-0019) is free and available 24/7.
around.source: The Times of India / NDTV / The Hindu

around.headline: Bengaluru police bust international drug trafficking network, seize ₹60.86 crore in narcotics
around.summary: City police arrested eight people and dismantled a cross-border narcotics network in one of the larger seizures reported this year. Case details are still being probed.

around.headline: Doctors link hours in Bengaluru traffic to migraines, stress and hearing loss
around.summary: A new report flags long-term health costs — migraines, stress and hearing damage — from prolonged exposure to the city's traffic and honking. If your commute is long, noise-cancelling headphones and periodic breaks can cut the exposure.

around.headline: Forecasters flag possible thundershowers and traffic disruption today
around.summary: Weather reports warned of intense thundershowers that could snarl traffic across the city today; the latest forecast has trimmed rain odds to 31% for the rest of the day, but it's worth checking conditions before you head out during peak hours.

around.headline: Street vendors push back on GBA eviction drive; CM promises rehabilitation
around.summary: Vendors have welcomed a fresh survey but oppose the eviction drive and main-road ban on their stalls; CM Shivakumar says they'll be rehabilitated rather than lose their livelihoods. Expect some disruption near main roads as this plays out.
around.source: The News Minute / The New Indian Express

around.headline: India pushes US to drop proposed 12.5% tariff in trade talks
around.summary: India is negotiating with Washington to scrap a proposed 12.5% tariff as part of ongoing bilateral trade talks. How this lands could ripple into IT/export pricing and consumer goods costs in the months ahead.

street_smart.title: You're parking your two-wheeler outside a shop for 'just two minutes.' What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Evergreen topic (rotated in): no single incident in today's feeds fit the trap-scenario format, and today's news already covered a restaurant-dispute scenario this morning. Two-wheeler theft prevention hasn't appeared in the last 3 weeks of briefs.
street_smart.situation: You've pulled up outside a shop to grab something quick. It'll only take a minute, the street looks busy enough, and unlocking your bike again on the way out feels like a hassle. What do you do?

choices.text: Leave it exactly as is — engine off, no lock engaged — since you'll be back in a minute.
choices.feedback: This is the exact window most Bengaluru two-wheeler thefts happen in: an unlocked handlebar can be forced straight and the bike pushed or ridden away in under 15 seconds — thieves specifically scan for bikes parked 'just for a minute' with no lock engaged.

choices.text: Engage only the factory steering/handlebar lock and walk in.
choices.feedback: Better than nothing, but a single steering lock can be forced by an experienced thief in under a minute, and the bike can still be lifted straight onto a waiting vehicle — a single point of security isn't much of a deterrent on its own.

choices.text: Park wherever's closest to the door, regardless of visibility, reasoning that daytime theft is rare.
choices.feedback: Daytime crowds don't deter opportunistic theft the way you'd think — a busy street actually gives a thief more cover to blend in immediately after. Visibility to shopkeepers or CCTV matters more than time of day.

choices.text: Engage the steering lock, add a disc or chain lock, and park within view of the shop's CCTV or a shopkeeper.
choices.feedback: Correct. Layering a second physical lock forces a thief to spend time cutting or carrying tools, and a visible, monitored spot adds the risk of being seen — together they push your bike out of the 'quick and easy' category most opportunistic thefts target.

takeaways: Use a second lock (disc or chain) in addition to the factory steering lock — thieves specifically target bikes secured at only one point.
If your bike is stolen, file an FIR immediately with the exact time and location — most CCTV footage in the area gets overwritten within 24-72 hours, so speed matters for recovery.
Keep a note of your bike's engine and chassis numbers somewhere separate from the bike itself — it speeds up both the FIR and any later insurance or recovery match.

quiz.q: According to today's reports, what happened at a home in Bengaluru's Kottigepalya?
options: A suspected murder-suicide within a family
quiz.why: Police say a man killed three family members before dying by suicide; the case remains under investigation.

quiz.q: Roughly how much in narcotics did Bengaluru police seize in today's international drug trafficking bust?
quiz.why: Eight people were arrested in what's being described as one of the larger such seizures reported this year.

quiz.q: What tariff rate is India pushing the US to drop in ongoing bilateral trade talks, per today's news?
quiz.why: The outcome could affect IT/export pricing and consumer goods costs in India in the months ahead.

quiz.q: Bengaluru sits primarily on which elevated plateau that covers much of peninsular India?
quiz.why: The Deccan Plateau's elevation is part of why Bengaluru enjoys a cooler climate than much of the rest of South India.

quiz.q: What does 'RBI', India's central banking institution, stand for?
quiz.why: The RBI regulates monetary policy and oversees banks, while SEBI — a separate body — regulates the stock markets.
